MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 60744930) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DOVGAN, VALENTYNA F

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VALENTYNA DOVGAN'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ONLY CONTAINS MS. DOVGAN'S SIGNATURE AND THE SIGNATURE OF HER ATTORNEY MARK ZANOBI (EXHIBITS TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION, EXH. 4, P. 2-3) AND IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE AGREEMENT WAS NEVER SIGNED BY DR. MIKHELSON OR HER INSURER. THIS FAILS TO COMPORT WITH THE REQUIREMENT THAT A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BE SIGNED BY BOTH THE PARTY SEEKING TO ENFORCE THE AGREEMENT UNDER CCP 664.6 AND THE PARTY AGAINST WHOM IT IS SOUGHT TO BE ENFORCED. (HARRIS V. RUDIN, RICHMAN & APPEL (1999) 74 CAL.APP.4TH 299, 305, 87; SULLY-MILLER CONTRACTING CO. V. GLEDSON/CASHMAN CONST., INC. (2002) 103 CAL.APP.4TH 30, 37.) STRICT COMPLIANCE WITH THE SIGNATURE REQUIREMENT IS A PREREQUISITE TO INVOKING THE POWER OF THE COURT TO IMPOSE A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DUE TO THE SUMMARY NATURE OF CCP 664.6. (SULLY-MILLER CONTRACTING, SUPRA, 103 CAL.APP.4TH AT P. 37.) THE DENTAL BOARD'S INVESTIGATION OF DEFENDANT AND THE WRITTEN CONSENT REQUIREMENT OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 801(F) ARE NOT RELEVANT TO RESOLVING THIS MOTION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF VALENTYNA DOVGAN's MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT is denied. The Settlement Agreement only contains Ms. Dovgan's signature and the signature of her attorney Mark Zanobi (Exhibits to Plaintiff's Motion, Exh. 4, p. 2-3) and it is undisputed that the agreement was never signed by Dr. Mikhelson or her insurer. This fails to comport with the requirement that a settlement agreement be signed by both the party seeking to enforce the agreement under CCP 664.6 and the party against whom it is sought to be enforced. (Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 305, 87; Sully-Miller Contracting Co. v. Gledson/Cashman Const., Inc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 30, 37.) Strict compliance with the signature requirement is a prerequisite to invoking the power of the court to impose a settlement agreement due to the summary nature of CCP 664.6. (Sully-Miller Contracting, supra, 103 Cal.App.4th at p. 37.) The Dental Board's investigation of defendant and the written consent requirement of Business & Professions Code 801(f) are not relevant to resolving this motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
